T.value: Lifetime of an electric motor (in years), with pdf f(t) = 0.25 t e^{-0.5 t}, t > 0
T.description: The random variable representing the lifetime of an electric motor, whose probability density function is f(t) = 0.25 t e^{-0.5 t} for t > 0. The answer integrates this pdf to find the warranty period.

warranty_failure_probability.description: The maximum proportion of motors allowed to require warranty services (3%), used as the threshold probability in solving for the warranty period.
